---

# AMOS — Internal Journey

The staff side of the couple's journey. What happens behind the scenes at each stage.

Every action below is triggered by a couple moving through their journey. Purely operational workflows (staffing, inventory, vendor management) are out of scope here.

---

## 1. Discovery

**Triggered by:** couple encounters Bolt Farm

Internal work:
- Attribution is captured automatically (UTM, referral source, ad campaign, call tracking)
- Marketing sees which channels are producing inquiries in real time
- No manual work — this stage is fully automated

---

## 2. Inquiry

**Triggered by:** couple submits a form, calls in, starts a chat, or responds to an ad

Internal work:
- A record is created in AMOS — the couple now exists in the system
- Inquiry is routed based on channel (inbound call → setter, form → setter, chat → available team member)
- Setter is tasked with making contact and booking a conversation
- If the couple came in via phone, call recording and attribution are automatically attached

Handoff: **Marketing → Setter**

---

## 3. Conversation

**Triggered by:** couple engages through any channel

Internal work:
- If sales call: closer receives the couple with full context (source, inquiry details, any prior interaction)
- If self-serve: the website guides the couple through availability, experience tiers, and investment framing
- Quotes are created with expiry dates
- Follow-up tasks auto-generate if the couple goes quiet
- If the couple doesn't book: they enter the nurturing flow (see 01 alternate paths)

Handoff: **Setter → Closer** (if sales-assisted) or **self-serve → system**

---

## 4. Commitment

**Triggered by:** deposit paid, reservation made

Internal work:
- Reservation is confirmed in AMOS
- Payment is recorded (deposit amount, method, Stripe reference)
- Deal properties are set (experience tier, villa, dates, investment amount, revenue, cash collected)
- Admin is tasked with reconciliation
- Upsell opportunity is created automatically
- Rebooking program deal is created automatically
- Confirmation is sent to the couple

Handoff: **Closer / System → Admin** (reconciliation) + **System → Guest Experience** (upsell)

---

## 5. Anticipation

**Triggered by:** time between commitment and arrival

Internal work:
- Guest experience team reaches out for upsell (spa, Romance Experience, VIP, meals)
- Concierge communication begins (arrival details, preferences, special requests)
- As arrival date approaches:
  - Housekeeping is tasked with villa preparation
  - Maintenance receives any pre-arrival work orders
  - Spa is notified of booked treatments and schedules therapists
  - Kitchen is notified of meal orders
  - Front desk receives the arrival manifest
- All prep tasks have owners and deadlines — unacknowledged items escalate

Handoff: **Guest Experience → Housekeeping, Maintenance, Spa, Kitchen, Front Desk**

---

## 6. Arrival & Exhale

**Triggered by:** couple arrives

Internal work:
- Front desk executes the arrival process
- Villa status is confirmed ready (housekeeping sign-off, maintenance sign-off)
- Couple is checked in — status updates across the system
- Any last-minute requests are routed immediately
- If anything is wrong with the villa: service recovery triggers instantly (see 01 alternate paths)

Handoff: **Front Desk → active monitoring by all on-site teams**

---

## 7. The Renewal

**Triggered by:** couple is on-site experiencing the renewal

Internal work:
- Spa executes booked treatments on schedule
- Kitchen delivers meals on schedule
- Housekeeping performs mid-experience service (turndown, refresh)
- Maintenance is on standby — any issue is routed and resolved immediately
- Front desk / concierge handles real-time requests
- On-site rebooking conversation happens (hosts)
- All interactions are logged — nothing is verbal-only

Handoff: **coordinated between Spa, Kitchen, Housekeeping, Maintenance, Front Desk, Hosts**

---

## 8. Return Home

**Triggered by:** couple departs

Internal work:
- Front desk executes departure process
- Final folio is settled (remaining balance, incidentals, gratuities)
- Villa is marked for turnover — housekeeping is tasked
- Maintenance receives post-departure inspection checklist
- If rebooking deposit was collected on-site: rebooking deal is updated

Handoff: **Front Desk → Housekeeping, Maintenance** (turnover) + **Front Desk → Finance** (folio settlement)

---

## 9. Afterglow

**Triggered by:** couple has departed

Internal work:
- Automated follow-up sequence begins (thank you, testimonial request)
- If testimonial is received: review for category-consistent language before publishing
- Re-engagement triggers are set (content, seasonal offers, anniversary reminders)
- Sales team monitors for rebooking signals
- 265-day anniversary task is set for the deal owner

Handoff: **System → Marketing** (re-engagement) + **System → Sales** (rebooking signals)

---

## 10. Rebooking

**Triggered by:** couple re-engages to book again

Internal work:
- Returning couple is recognized — full history is available (previous experience, preferences, spend)
- Rebooking program perks are applied automatically
- The couple re-enters Stage 3 (Conversation) or Stage 4 (Commitment) depending on how they rebook
- Past guest pipeline is activated

Handoff: **Sales / System → same flow as stages 3–9, with returning couple context**

---

## 11. Evangelist

**Triggered by:** couple refers others or shares publicly

Internal work:
- Referral is attributed to the originating couple
- New couple enters Stage 1 (Discovery) with referral source attached
- Originating couple's value is tracked (lifetime value + referred revenue)

Handoff: **System → Marketing / Sales** (new referred couple enters the journey)

---

## Alternate Paths — Internal Side

### Didn't Book
**Branches from:** Stage 3 (Conversation)

Internal work:
- Close-loss reason is recorded (required, not optional)
- Couple moves to nurturing pipeline automatically
- Setter is tasked with follow-up cadence
- Marketing adds couple to re-engagement sequences (email, retargeting)
- If the couple re-engages later: full context from original conversation is available, not starting from scratch

Handoff: **Closer → Setter** (follow-up) + **System → Marketing** (nurturing sequences)

---

### Cancellation / Recovery
**Branches from:** Stage 4 (Commitment) or Stage 5 (Anticipation)

Internal work:
- Cancellation/reschedule request is logged with reason
- Original deal owner is tasked immediately — recover call is the priority
- Reschedule is attempted first:
  - New dates selected → reservation updated, no revenue lost
  - Rescheduling fee applied if applicable
- If cancellation proceeds:
  - Refund policy is applied (48-hr cancel, 50% policy at 45 days, extenuating circumstances)
  - Refund amount and method are recorded
  - Finance is tasked with processing the refund
  - Upsell and rebooking deals associated with this couple are cleaned up
- If any revenue is retained: deal stays Closed Won for reporting
- Couple enters nurturing flow — the relationship is not over

Handoff: **System → Deal Owner** (recovery) → **Deal Owner → Admin** (reconciliation) → **Admin → Finance** (refund if needed)

---

### No-Show

#### Sales No-Show
**Branches from:** Stage 3 (Conversation)

Internal work:
- Deal is moved to No-Show status
- Setter is automatically tasked to re-engage and rebook the call
- If rebooked: couple re-enters Stage 3
- If unresponsive after follow-up cadence: couple moves to nurturing

Handoff: **System → Setter** (chase)

#### Arrival No-Show
**Branches from:** Stage 6 (Arrival)

Internal work:
- Front desk flags the no-show when couple doesn't arrive within the expected window
- Outreach is attempted (phone, SMS, email)
- If unreachable: management is notified
- Financial handling follows cancellation policy
- Villa is released back to available inventory after defined window
- Housekeeping turnover is cancelled or adjusted

Handoff: **Front Desk → Management** (decision) → **Admin → Finance** (financial handling)

---

### Service Recovery
**Branches from:** Stage 6 (Arrival) or Stage 7 (The Renewal)

Internal work:
- Issue is reported (by staff or by couple) and logged immediately
- Maintenance or responsible team is tasked with immediate resolution — this is highest priority
- Management is notified in real time
- Resolution is tracked: what went wrong, response time, outcome
- Compensation decision is made (upgrade, comp item, future credit, partial refund)
- Compensation is recorded on the folio
- Post-resolution check-in with the couple to confirm the experience is back on track
- Incident feeds into operational KPIs — patterns are surfaced (recurring issues, specific villas, specific teams)

Handoff: **Reporting party → Maintenance/Responsible team** (fix) + **System → Management** (awareness) + **Management → Front Desk/Concierge** (couple communication)

---

### Gifting
**Branches from:** Stage 1 (Discovery)

Two internal tracks:

**Buyer track:**
- Buyer enters the system as a contact (not a couple)
- Conversation and commitment follow the standard path but with gift framing
- Payment is collected from the buyer
- Gift notification is prepared (delivery method, timing, personalization)
- Buyer journey ends at commitment — they don't experience the renewal

**Recipient track:**
- Recipient couple is created in AMOS when the gift is delivered/redeemed
- They enter at Stage 5 (Anticipation) — commitment is already made for them
- Concierge reaches out to coordinate dates and preferences
- From here, the standard internal journey applies (stages 5–11)
- Attribution links back to the buyer for referral tracking

Handoff: **Sales → System** (gift setup) → **System → Concierge** (recipient onboarding)

---

### Proposal
**Branches from:** Stage 1 (Discovery)

Internal work:
- Single person booking — flagged as proposal experience in the system
- Higher coordination requirement than standard reservation:
  - Surprise element must be protected (all communication goes to the booker only)
  - Custom staging requirements (decor, timing, photographer if requested)
  - Detailed run-of-show is created and shared with on-site team
- Multiple teams coordinate: Sales (booking), Concierge (planning), Housekeeping (staging), Spa (if included), Front Desk (arrival choreography)
- On the day: run-of-show is executed with precision
- After the proposal: couple may convert to the standard journey for a return renewal (rebooking opportunity)

Handoff: **Sales → Concierge** (planning) → **Concierge → Housekeeping, Spa, Front Desk** (execution)

---

## Key Principle

At every stage, the internal journey must satisfy the foundation principle: **every task and handoff has an owner and a deadline — unacknowledged items escalate automatically.** If a handoff is missed, AMOS catches it before the couple feels it.
